target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 — that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** — deterministic key per pinned tab (`…:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** — each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** — render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `→ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** — only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** — reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** — re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The node-bridge driver uses its own literal `extension-bridge`, distinct from the legacy `extension` → `existing-session` value (whose doctor migration is preserved), so there is no deprecation collision.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed — superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

Local + public ClawSweeper / Codex review rounds flagged the items below; all are resolved or explained:

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build — `no-token → 1008`, `wrong-token → 1008`, `correct-HMAC → accepted` — plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[high] node-event emission on the public SDK** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) — **fixed** (`04d6d08b3b`). `emitNodeGatewayEvent` (and the install primitives) are removed from the public plugin-SDK barrel and moved to a dedicated **private** entrypoint `node-gateway-events-internal` (mirroring `ssrf-runtime-internal`): built for the bundled first-party bridge but excluded from public package exports, so a third-party plugin cannot install an emitter or forge node-attributed events. The extension package-boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-internal`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`, `subpaths-exported`) all pass. (The private-subpath form as first implemented broke **runtime** resolution — found via live deploy, fixed in `b3e840c785`; see #14.)
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) — **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collided with the legacy migration** — **fixed** (`677c875a8e`). The branch had repurposed the legacy `driver:"extension"` literal (which the doctor migrates to `existing-session`, Chrome MCP attach) for the new node-bridge mode *and* deleted that migration, so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode profile and legacy upgrades broke. Fixed by giving the new mode its own literal **`extension-bridge`** (zod union + refine, public type, the input-side resolver, the macOS toggle) and **restoring** the deleted `extension → existing-session` profile migration. `doctor-legacy-config` migration test passes; build clean. (The rename initially left the relay gate on the dead `extension` literal — found via live deploy, fixed in `f4f56efa2e`; see #15.)
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph change** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) — **stacked-base drift; rebase/approval-gated.** The bundled `chrome-extension/package.json` is `private` and adds **no runtime dependencies**; the only lockfile changes are upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR. They disappear on the rebase to the extension-only delta. Full resolution needs that rebase or maintainer dependency-guard approval — neither available pre-#93411.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** — **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.
8. **[P2] relay URL throws under tokenless auth** (`background-utils.js`) — **fixed** (`e1d56a8aba`). `buildRelayWsUrl` threw on a blank gateway token, but `gateway.auth.mode:"none"` is valid; it now returns a tokenless `/extension` URL (the node bridge accepts it only when it also has no configured token, so a token-protected bridge still rejects it).
9. **[P1] Schema-invalid macOS profile** (`ConfigStore.swift:178`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). The My Browser toggle wrote `{driver:"extension-bridge"}` but the zod profile schema requires `color`; the toggle now writes a `color` so the saved config validates. (Distinct from #4, which added `priorDefaultProfile`.)
10. **[P1] Copilot prompt dropped** (`server-node-events.ts`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `server-lifecycle` emits `extraSystemPrompt` on the node-originated `agent.request`, but the receiver's `AgentDeepLink` parser omitted the field, so the browser-copilot persona never reached the agent. Added `extraSystemPrompt` to the parsed type + the `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch input (which already threads it into the run).
11. **[P1] Explicit relay host pointed at the gateway** (`background.js getRelayHost`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `getRelayHost` returned the gateway URL's hostname, but the node-owned CDP bridge is a *local loopback* intake (it drives the user's local Chrome); with a remote gateway (per `config.example`'s `wss://…ts.net`) the relay would dial the wrong host. It now defaults to `127.0.0.1`, with an explicit `relayHost` override for advanced setups.
12. **[high] Node-routing fail-open** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). A failed node turn silently fell back to a direct (unconfined) `sessions.send`, dropping `gateway.tools.byNode` confinement. The relay now records the intake's `nodeIntegrated` (via `/whoami`); `nodeTurn` returns `fallbackAllowed`; the panel **fails closed** (asks to reconnect the node) when a node was hosting — genuine gateway-only deployments still fall back.
13. **[P2] Options page blocked tokenless loopback** (`options.js`) — **fixed** (`c99fd6ab78`). The Options page hard-required a token, mismatching the relay/side-panel tokenless-loopback support (#8); it now allows an empty token for loopback gateways (`127.0.0.1`/`localhost`/`::1`) while still requiring one for remote gateways.
14. **[P0] F3 seam broke runtime module resolution — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`b3e840c785`). The F3 fix (#3) registered `node-gateway-events-internal` in `plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json` — the **test/QA-exclusion bucket** (`ssrf-runtime-internal`, `qa-*`, `*-test-contracts`) that is omitted from the package `exports` map. In any **installed** package the bridge's runtime `import` threw `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`, so `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never bound the relay (the error was swallowed by its own `try/catch`) and node-anchored My Browser silently failed. Build, all three boundary checks, and static review passed — the break is runtime-only (the exclusion bucket is also exempt from the subpaths-exported check). Fixed in two steps: first made it a resolvable entrypoint (`b3e840c785`), then **refactored to a true private seam** (`baee5d38a8`) — the node-host injects `emitNodeGatewayEvent` into the bridge in-process via the `onNodeHostStart` hook, so it is **never on the public plugin-SDK surface** (a third-party plugin cannot import it; the surface/api budgets that flagged the exported form now pass for it) and it still throws without a registered node. `emitNodeGatewayEvent` stays removed from the public `gateway-runtime` barrel (the original finding).
15. **[P0] F4 rename left the relay gate on a dead literal — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`f4f56efa2e`). `ResolvedBrowserConfig.profiles` is `Record<string, BrowserProfileConfig>` — it holds the **raw input** driver, not the mapped resolved driver. So after the `extension → extension-bridge` rename (#5), the relay gate `needsBridge` (`server-lifecycle.ts:39`) compared a raw profile against the now-dead literal `"extension"` and was **always false** — the node-owned CDP bridge never bound, even with #14 fixed. Gate on the input `"extension-bridge"`. (`config.ts:552` and `profile-capabilities.ts:27` are correct — they operate on the mapped/resolved driver.) Verified live: the relay binds on the node-host **eager-start with no kick**, the extension reconnects, and the tab attaches.
16. **[high] Node-turn status failure could bypass confinement** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`12b9e03fa1`). The `catch` around the `nodeTurn` message treated a *rejected* `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` (background worker/relay unreachable) the same as a gateway-only deployment and fell through to a direct, unconfined `sessions.send`. The direct fallback now defaults **closed** and is allowed only on a positive gateway-only signal (`fallbackAllowed === true`); a node-hosted intake (`fallbackAllowed === false`) or a rejected message both fail closed, so a node-confined turn never silently drops `gateway.tools.byNode`.
17. **[high] node identity not surfaced on the bridge `/whoami`** (`server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`1ce3dccc87`). `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never passed an identity to `ensureExtensionBridge`, so `/whoami` always reported `nodeIntegrated:false`; the extension then treated a node-hosted bridge as gateway-only and allowed the fallback above. The hosting node's identity is now threaded through the DI seam (`runner → onNodeHostStart ctx → registry → ensureExtensionBridge`); verified live: `/whoami` returns `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` (+ `extension-bridge-server.whoami.test.ts`).
18. **[P1] browser CLI status didn't surface the `extension-bridge` driver** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`, refined `fc4fac3a29`). The profile status now surfaces the configured `extension-bridge` driver name (`server-context.ts` reverse-maps the resolved `extension` 1:1; `ProfileStatus.driver` type updated). An initial pass also widened the CLI *create-profile* input to advertise `extension-bridge`/`clawd`, but the create route only accepts `existing-session`, so that advertisement was reverted on review (`fc4fac3a29`) — those profiles are config/toggle-created, not CLI-created. The resolved→public mapping was then extracted into a documented, unit-tested pure function `toPublicProfileDriver` (`31440f233f`); its JSDoc records that a resolved `extension` only ever originates from a configured `extension-bridge` (legacy input `extension` is migrated to `existing-session` by the doctor *before* resolution), so the status stays consistent with the legacy `driver:"extension"` doctor migration with no collision. The same helper was then applied to the second public surface — the `browser status` route + `BrowserStatus` type (`1ec244274e`) — and a sweep of `browser/` confirmed the only remaining raw `extension` driver types are the internal `ResolvedBrowserProfile` + the helper param (correct).
19. **[P2] `extension-bridge` not documented in generated config help** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`). The zod `driver` union had no `.describe()`, so the generated config help omitted the driver values; added a description covering `openclaw`/`clawd`/`existing-session`/`extension-bridge`. (Build + config-help-quality test + browser-tool tests green.)
20. **[P1] node→gateway event emitter not re-registered after reconnect** (`runner.ts`) — **fixed** (`fc4fac3a29`). `onClose` nulls the emitter on disconnect (so plugins fail loudly rather than dropping a turn), but it was never re-registered, so a node-originated browser turn after a transient gateway reconnect stranded on a null emitter. Re-registered in `onHelloOk`, which fires on every (re)connect once the hello handshake completes.
21. **[medium] unauthenticated loopback CDP bridge face** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **hardened** (`b3533a10c5`). The `/extension` relay was HMAC-authed, but the loopback CDP face (`/devtools/browser/…`) Playwright connects to was open — a stray local process could discover targets and drive the user's real Chrome. The bridge now registers its token in the bridge-auth registry and requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the `/devtools/browser/` upgrade (constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual`); with no token configured it stays trusted-local (back-compat). `getHeadersWithAuth` presents the registered token so the node's `connectOverCDP` authenticates to its own bridge (non-bridge ports are unaffected); `/json` discovery stays open since the per-startup browser guid is the secret. Covered by `extension-bridge-server.cdp-auth.test.ts` and **verified E2E on a live node-anchored deploy — the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org with the CDP face now authenticated (no regression):**

![CDP-face auth E2E — the LLM agent drives the attached tab to example.org ("Done — the tab is now on https://www.example.org/") with the loopback CDP face now requiring the bridge token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/cdp-auth-e2e.png)
22. **[high] node-gateway emitter exposed on the public plugin SDK** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`0913003ee2`, finalized `efa5cbd4c4`). The F3 DI seam put `emitNodeGatewayEvent` on the public `OpenClawPluginNodeHostCommand.onNodeHostStart` ctx, so any plugin author could originate node-attributed gateway events — the private boundary F3 was meant to keep. The emitter is removed from the public `onNodeHostStart` ctx type (which now carries only `nodeId`); the node-host passes it on the **runtime** ctx **only to the bundled node-anchored browser bridge**, gated on the **registry plugin identity** (`entry.pluginId === "browser"`, the bundled plugin's manifest id — not a forgeable self-declared cap; `9a82c1d790`) — every other plugin's startup hook gets just `nodeId`; the emitter stays off the public `{nodeId?}` ctx type (the browser plugin reads it via an internal cast). (Three interim passes — a host-internal `plugin-sdk` subpath, an ungated runtime ctx, then a cap-gated ctx — were superseded to drop a package self-resolution, stop the emitter being visible to all plugin hooks, and gate on identity rather than a declarable cap.) Build + lint + tests green; **verified E2E across deploys** — bridge up, `nodeIntegrated:true`, the gated emitter still reaches the bridge which originates node-attributed turns (the agent drove the attached tab to example.net → example.com → iana.org).
23. **[P2] stale byNode tests (run-scoped contract)** — **fixed** (`b58b000d71`). The byNode tests (from the stacked #93411 base) targeted the retired session-global registry (`setSessionHostingNodeId`) instead of the run-scoped `hostingNodeId`, so they failed against the final implementation: byNode is enforced in `createOpenClawCodingTools` (`agent-tools.ts:932`, `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(options.hostingNodeId)`) where `hostingNodeId` is threaded through the run (`attempt.ts → params → agent-tools`); `resolveGatewayScopedTools` is a byNode no-op for non-node MCP/HTTP callers. The embedded test now passes `hostingNodeId` (where byNode bites); the gateway-resolver test is rewritten to a single no-op assertion. Both pass (68/68 + green). *(These are #93411 stacked-base test files — fixed here per request; they reconcile on the #93411 rebase.)*

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot — node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` — before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ — "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword → tab B asked for it → "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7→2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke ✓` ×3 → `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected — Internet Assigned N…" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) — proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix — re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` → unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.

**Verbatim run logs (latest build, redacted):**

```
# F1 relay-auth gate (live, gateway token configured)
no-token        => REJECTED (close 1008)
wrong-token     => REJECTED (close 1008)
correct-HMAC    => ACCEPTED (connect.challenge)

# Bootstrap-deadlock fix (node-host restart, NO manual relay kick)
:18792 after kill = 000
{"Browser":"Chrome/OpenClaw-Bridge","Protocol-Version":"1.3","webSocketDebuggerUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:18792/devtools/browser/<id>"}

# Node-hop turn (side-panel -> node -> attached Chrome tab)
[gateway] agent ... node=ffc1b7d3...        # turn attributed to the hosting node
node.invoke OK x3 (browser.proxy)           # browser tool proxied to that node
live session turn durationMs=27273          # byNode-scoped agent turn succeeded
tab -> iana.org ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority")

# F3 host-internal seam (extension package-boundary checks)
no-plugin-sdk-internal       : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
no-src-outside-plugin-sdk    : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
subpaths-exported            : OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported.
```

**Proof artifacts:** the full redacted run logs above are also collected in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/17fba6011e351775e97d8b21a0d67ab7). Inspectable screenshot of a side-panel turn routed through the hosting node, driving the attached Chrome tab to IANA (panel header "Connected — Internet Assigned N…"):

![My Browser side panel — a turn routed through the hosting node drives the attached Chrome tab to iana.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/node_e2e2.png)

**Latest build (after the F3+F4 P0 fixes) — the LLM agent drives the user's real attached tab end-to-end:** on a clean install the relay binds on the node-host eager-start (no kick), the bundled extension reconnects and attaches the live tab, and side-panel turns are handled by the agent itself — routed through the hosting node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`, `live session turn durationMs=7211`), the agent calls its browser tool on the attached tab and replies in the panel. Below: a multi-turn session navigates the live tab `wikipedia.org → github.com` from side-panel turns ("Go to wikipedia.org" → "Now take me to github.com"), each confirmed by the agent (the panel shows "You're now on github.com.").

![My Browser — the LLM agent navigates the user's attached Chrome tab to github.com from a side-panel turn, routed through the hosting node](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-agent.gif)

**Comprehensive re-verification (recorded on `1ce3dccc87`, after the node-identity fix; the only later commits (`12b9e03fa1` fail-closed hardening, `c03c0c3585` driver surfacing, `fc4fac3a29` reconnect emitter re-registration + create-input revert, `31440f233f` driver-mapping extract/test, `1ec244274e` BrowserStatus mapping, `b3533a10c5` CDP-face auth, `0913003ee2`/`efa5cbd4c4`/`fe25b016f8`/`9a82c1d790` emitter off public SDK, identity-gated to the browser bridge; `b58b000d71` byNode test repair) do not change these successful-turn behaviors):** `/whoami` now reports `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` on the node-hosted bridge, so the D-fix fail-closed actually fires.

**🎥 Live video on the latest-head runtime (after the full 12-round review chain, identity-gated emitter + run-scoped byNode + CDP-face auth all live):** the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab from a side-panel turn — typed prompt → `→ browser` → the tab navigates live → *"Done — now on https://www.wikipedia.org/"*:

![Live video — the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab to wikipedia.org from a side-panel turn, on the latest-head runtime (node-routed, identity-gated emitter)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-latest-head.gif)

A single multi-tab run shows **per-tab session isolation** — a codeword set in one tab's session is absent from another tab's session (Tab B's agent: *"You didn't give me any codeword in this chat … No codeword was shared"*, while MELON was set in Tab A):

![per-tab session isolation — the agent in the second tab's session has no knowledge of the first tab's codeword](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/per-tab-isolation.png)

![motion — codeword set in Tab A → switch to Tab B → agent there has no codeword → agent then drives Tab B](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-pertab.gif)

Each side-panel turn routes through the node on this head (`node.invoke ✓` browser.proxy + `claude live session turn` per turn). **byNode** confines a node turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode = {…ffc1b7d3…: {allow:[browser,web_search]}}`; the before→after narrowing (`[byNode] … before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{…}`, above) is captured against the **native agent runtime**. Honest note: the live test box runs the **claude-cli** backend, which sources its tools via MCP rather than the gateway's native tool resolver, so I could not re-surface that before→after on this box — the native-runtime capture stands, and byNode coverage of claude-cli backends is a `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411-primitive) question, not this PR's routing change.

> **Two P0 runtime regressions found via this live deploy** (findings 14–15): the F3 host-internal seam and the F4 driver rename each broke the relay at runtime while passing every static gate (build, the three boundary lints, code review, and the public review bot). A real-behavior deploy on a clean install is what surfaced them.

- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge — it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular → node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the node→gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension-bridge"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) — the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` — forwards `agent.request` → `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` — pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes — turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* → bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* → only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* → idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured; **two P0 runtime regressions (F3 export resolution, F4 relay gate) found via live deploy and fixed** (`b3e840c785`, `f4f56efa2e`); on the fixed build the relay binds on the node-host eager-start with **no kick**, the bundled extension reconnects + attaches, and the **LLM agent drives the user's attached tab from side-panel turns** (`wikipedia.org → github.com`, recording embedded). F3's final form is the in-process DI seam (`baee5d38a8`).

target.title: fix(tavily): fall back to process.env when config SecretRef is unresolvable
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`tavily_search` and `tavily_extract` fail at runtime with `unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY"` even when `TAVILY_API_KEY` is set in the gateway process environment. The configured `SecretRef` points at an env-backed credential that the active runtime snapshot can't inline-resolve, but the plugin's own call chain already has a `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` fallback — `normalizeConfiguredSecret` in strict mode throws before that fallback can run. Bundled peers (`brave`, `firecrawl`, `exa`) use `readConfiguredSecretString` from `extensions/web-search-provider-common` and don't hit this; tavily and exa are the only remaining `extensions/*/src/config.ts` helpers still throwing in strict mode.

## Evidence

**Environment:** Linux, Node v24, OpenClaw `v2026.6.8` (commit `8a24f93`), with the patch applied via `prestart.sh` step 7/7 in `kzzalews/mikrus-gitops` (commit `ab70875`).

**Reproduction (before fix):**

```bash
export TAVILY_API_KEY=***
openclaw config set plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey \
  --ref-provider default --ref-source env --ref-id TAVILY_API_KEY
openclaw infer web search --provider tavily --query ping --limit 1 --json
# → Error: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
```

**After this patch:** Same command reaches Tavily. With a dummy key, the expected downstream error is `401 Unauthorized: missing or invalid API key` (auth failure, not SecretRef failure). With a live key, the call returns search results. Observed round-trip ~1.3s for `tavily_search`.

**Test coverage added (`extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts`):**

- returns the configured string `apiKey` as-is (happy path)
- falls back to `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` when the configured env SecretRef is unresolvable AND its id is `TAVILY_API_KEY`
- does **NOT** fall back when the configured SecretRef is a file source (regression coverage for the silent-override scenario)
- does **NOT** fall back when the configured env SecretRef targets a different env var (regression coverage)
- returns `undefined` when neither config nor env var is set

**What was not tested:** A clean from-source `pnpm test` from a fresh checkout — patch was validated against a compiled `dist/` of `v2026.6.8` and a manually-patched version of the same file. CI re-validates from source on every push.

## Real behavior proof (2026-06-21, isolated test pod on k3s `dawid135`)

Re-ran a clean BEFORE/AFTER against a fresh test pod with the
runtime `ab70875` sed-patch **deliberately disabled** so the
upstream unpatched dist file is exercised. Config used the same
`{source: env, provider: default, id: TAVILY_API_KEY}` shape that
production ships in `openclaw.json`.

### BEFORE — unpatched dist (md5 `0f523c6c5d868b72727edcfb0cfeac3c`)

Synthetic direct-module call (Node ESM import of the bundled
`tavily-client-Dh_YnIM9.js`):

```
$ kubectl -n openclaw exec openclaw-test-... -- node /tmp/repro.mjs

=== ERROR (no fix) ===
name=UnresolvedSecretInputError
message=plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
path="plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey"
```

Real LLM-driven MCP tool calls captured from the test pod's gateway log
on 2026-06-21T12:05:16Z, when an agent tried to research Tailscale and
the MCP `tavily_search` / `tavily_extract` tools failed for the same
reason:

```
2026-06-21T12:05:16.450+00:00 [tools] tavily_search failed: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it. raw_params={"query":"Tailscale official documentation site tailnet ACL MagicDNS subnet router exit node","max_results":10,"include_answer":true}
2026-06-21T12:05:16.462+00:00 [tools] tavily_search failed: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it. raw_params={"query":"Tailscale pricing 2026 Personal Pro Enterprise plans devices users","max_results":8,"include_answer":true}
2026-06-21T12:05:16.549+00:00 [tools] tavily_search failed: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it. raw_params={"query":"Tailscale features MagicDNS HTTPS TLS certificates HTTPS Let's Encrypt 2025","max_results":8,"include_answer":true}
2026-06-21T12:05:16.553+00:00 [tools] tavily_search failed: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it. raw_params={"query":"Tailscale Kubernetes operator k3s integration GitOps GitHub","max_results":6}
2026-06-21T12:05:29.366+00:00 [tools] tavily_extract failed: plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "env:default:TAVILY_API_KEY". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it. raw_params={"urls":["https://tailscale.com/","https://tailscale.com/kb/"],"query":"Tailscale documentation features overview","chunks_per_source":3}
```

This matches the symptom described in the original Evidence section
verbatim, on a freshly built `2026.6.8` image with **no runtime
patch** applied.

### AFTER — patched dist (md5 `0df2358612ec023b104fa90510fdb887`)

Same test pod, same Node script, after re-patching
`/app/dist/tavily-client-Dh_YnIM9.js` in place with the same 6-line
try/catch change that PR #95110 adds to source (and that the
`ab70875` runtime sed-patch applies to dist):

```
$ kubectl -n openclaw exec openclaw-test-... -- node /tmp/repro.mjs

=== OK (fix active) ===
{
  "ok": true,
  "tookMs": 1099,
  "count": 1,
  "first_title": "Ping (networking utility) - Wikipedia",
  "first_url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ping_(networking_utility)"
}
```

The patched `normalizeConfiguredSecret` swallows the strict-mode
`UnresolvedSecretInputError` and returns `""`, which lets
`resolveTavilyApiKey`'s `|| normalizeSecretInput(process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY)`
fallback rescue the key from the gateway's own environment.

### Dist diff (the exact change)

```diff
 function normalizeConfiguredSecret(value, path) {
-	return normalizeSecretInput(normalizeResolvedSecretInputString({
-		value,
-		path
-	}));
+	try {
+		return normalizeSecretInput(normalizeResolvedSecretInputString({
+			value,
+			path
+		}));
+	} catch {
+		// SecretRef could not be resolved against the active runtime snapshot
+		// (strict mode throws). Fall through to the process.env chain so the
+		// caller's `|| normalizeSecretInput(process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY)` can
+		// still pick up the key. Matches the pattern used by brave/firecrawl.
+		return "";
+	}
 }
```

### md5 reference

| State | md5 of `/app/dist/tavily-client-Dh_YnIM9.js` |
|---|---|
| Unpatched (upstream dist, bug present) | `0f523c6c5d868b72727edcfb0cfeac3c` |
| Patched (matches production) | `0df2358612ec023b104fa90510fdb887` |

The production pod currently sits on the patched md5 because
`ab70875` re-runs the sed-patch in `prestart.sh` on every container
start. The test pod ran on the unpatched md5 to expose the original
failure mode.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations (tavily web-search plugin)

## Linked Issue

Fixes a regression where the bundled tavily plugin (added in PR #49200) cannot fall back to `TAVILY_API_KEY` when the configured `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey` is a SecretRef the active runtime snapshot doesn't inline-resolve. Related closed issues that did not catch this runtime-side gap:

- #76491 — closed as "implemented on current main", but the vendored tavily/exa helpers (`extensions/tavily/src/config.ts`, `extensions/exa/src/config.ts`) still throw in strict mode.
- #82621 — closed by PR #82798 for the CLI `infer web search` path; this PR fixes the runtime plugin path that #82798 left open.

## Approach (v2, replaces earlier try/catch)

Replaces the previous `try/catch` approach with the inspect-mode pattern already used in `extensions/firecrawl/src/config.ts`:

1. Calls `resolveSecretInputString` with `mode: "inspect"` so unresolved SecretRefs return `configured_unavailable` instead of throwing.

The vendored tavily plugin's `normalizeConfiguredSecret` (in `extensions/tavily/src/config.ts`) called `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString({ value, path })` directly. With `resolveSecretInputString`'s default `mode: "strict"`, this throws `UnresolvedSecretInputError` when the active runtime snapshot cannot inline-resolve the SecretRef. The throw escaped before the caller's `|| normalizeSecretInput(process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY)` chain ran.

The companion helper `readConfiguredSecretString` in `extensions/web-search-provider-common/src/web-search-provider-common.ts` (used by `brave`, `firecrawl`, `exa`, and other bundled providers) wraps the same call in a function-local error path that returns `undefined` on the same failure. That mismatch is why brave/firecrawl/exa work and tavily does not.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — behavior change is limited to the previously-broken unresolvable-env-ref case; success path is identical.
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** a stricter upstream caller relies on `normalizeConfiguredSecret` throwing so it can surface the error to the user.
  - **Mitigation:** the only caller in this file (`resolveTavilyApiKey`) already has `process.env` and downstream-string fallbacks. The `||` chain in `resolveTavilyApiKey` was always meant to catch a `""` return from `normalizeConfiguredSecret`; the throw was incidental.
- **Risk:** a future plugin re-introduces the same throw pattern.
  - **Mitigation:** the new test in `extensions/tavily/src/config.test.ts` asserts the no-throw behavior, so any regression is caught at unit-test time.
- **Risk:** a future user configures a non-`TAVILY_API_KEY` env SecretRef (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY` for tavily) and the ambient `process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY` is silently used.
  - **Mitigation:** the new helper treats such refs as `blocked` and explicitly returns `undefined`; the new test pins this behavior.

* * *

## Generated with assistance from Aiko (AikoOpenClawBot), an AI agent running on OpenClaw 2026.6.8

- **Model:** `minimax/minimax-m3` via Kilo Gateway
- **Provider:** Kilo Gateway (`https://api.kilo.ai/api/gateway`)
- **Author:** Karol Zalewski [karol.zalewski@4zal.net](mailto:karol.zalewski@4zal.net)
- **Local reproduction:**
  - Patch + script: [kzzalews/openclaw#e520657](https://github.com/kzzalews/openclaw/commit/e520657)
  - Runtime SHA-baselined application: [kzzalews/mikrus-gitops#ab70875](https://github.com/kzzalews/mikrus-gitops/commit/ab70875)
  - Isolated test pod reproduction (2026-06-21): see `kzzalews/mikrus-gitops#a6572e9` discussion and the `PR-95110-reproduction-proof.md` artifact archived on the test PVC.

target.title: fix(agents): deliver background exec completion to agent via [OpenClaw exec completion]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: When a background exec command (`exec background=true`) completes, the agent receives a heartbeat wake with `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` as the transcript placeholder. This causes the model to treat the turn as a heartbeat poll — it reads `HEARTBEAT.md` and either runs periodic tasks or replies `HEARTBEAT_OK`, ignoring the exec completion details.
- **Why now**: Background exec is the primary async mechanism for long-running shell commands. The completion notification was effectively invisible to the model, defeating the purpose of async exec.
- **Outcome**: 
  - Exec completion events now use a dedicated `[OpenClaw exec completion]` transcript placeholder with the exec details appended in the message body, making them visible to both the model and `before_agent_run` security plugins.
  - When `deliverToUser` is false, `buildExecEventPrompt` now shows exec output with "Handle the result internally. Continue the task based on the result if needed" instead of "HEARTBEAT_OK only" — so the model sees completion details in any active conversation without overriding delivery authorization.
- **Intentionally out of scope**: Non-exec heartbeat behavior is unchanged.
- **Success**: The model now responds to exec completion with the command output (e.g. "The background command has finished. Here's the output:\n\n**first done**\n\n✅ Completed successfully (exit code 0)") instead of reading `HEARTBEAT.md` and replying `HEARTBEAT_OK`.
- **Reviewer focus**: 
  - Security gate visibility: exec details are in the message body (`[OpenClaw exec completion]\n\n...`), not in a separate `custom_message`, so `before_agent_run` hooks and security plugins can inspect the full content.
  - The `isExecCompletionUserMessage` filter uses `startsWith("EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT\n")` to handle the new body format.
  - `deliverToUser` is `params.canRelayToUser` only — no override. Instead, `buildExecEventPrompt`'s `!deliverToUser` branch now shows exec output with "handle internally, continue task" instructions.
  - The `lastChannel` parameter has been removed from `resolveHeartbeatRunPrompt`.

## Linked context

No related issue directly — discovered while investigating exec background completion notification behavior.

Related:
- The existing `HEARTBEAT_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT` ("[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]") pattern established the transcript placeholder filtering mechanism that this PR extends for exec completions.

## ClawSweeper feedback addressed

### P1/P2: Preserve internal-only exec delivery

The original P1 finding flagged hard-coding `deliverToUser: true` as bypassing the `target: "none"` delivery contract. Subsequent reviews flagged that raw `lastChannel` should not override delivery authorization. This PR addresses both concerns by separating exec event visibility from delivery authorization within `buildExecEventPrompt`:

**`deliverToUser` stays tied to canonical delivery:**

`deliverToUser: params.canRelayToUser` — no override. The `lastChannel` parameter has been removed from `resolveHeartbeatRunPrompt`. For webchat (where `canRelayToUser` is false because there's no `to` address), `deliverToUser` is `false`.

**Exec output is visible regardless of `deliverToUser`:**

When `deliverToUser` is `false`, `buildExecEventPrompt` no longer says "reply HEARTBEAT_OK only" or "do not mention, summarize, or reuse command output." Instead, it includes the completion details with output and instructs the model to "Handle the result internally. Continue the task based on the result if needed." This way:
- The model sees exec output in the active conversation and can respond naturally
- No `deliverToUser` override is needed
- Explicit `target: "none"` is naturally respected (deliverToUser stays false, no special check needed)
- Non-webchat channels with `target: "none"` also get the same improved prompt

**Why this approach:**

`canRelayToUser` checks whether the delivery system can actively push a message through the channel's addressing system (requires both `channel` and `to`). Background exec completion in an active webchat session is a different scenario — the message doesn't need external delivery; the model is already in the conversation and just needs to see the result. Rather than overriding `deliverToUser` to bypass this distinction, the prompt itself is adapted to show context without instructing relay.

**Security gate visibility:**

Unlike PR #91817 (which was closed because `before_agent_run` security plugins couldn't see exec details hidden in `custom_message`), this PR puts exec details directly in the message body via `transcriptBody = "${EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT}\n\n${effectiveBaseBody}"`. Security plugins inspecting user messages will see `[OpenClaw exec completion]\n\nExec completed (...)` — the full content the model receives, not just a placeholder.

**Why exec details persist in transcriptBody:**

Background exec is transparent to the user — the command runs asynchronously and completes without the user's direct involvement. When it finishes, the result needs to be visible in the ongoing conversation so the user can see what happened and the model can reference it in follow-up turns. An ephemeral-only design (visible on the current turn then discarded) would mean:

- The user could never scroll back to see what a background command produced
- The model would lose context of completed work after a single turn
- Debugging failed background commands would require external logs

The `[OpenClaw exec completion]` marker makes these entries distinguishable from regular user messages, and the existing `filterHeartbeatTranscriptArtifacts` mechanism already handles cleanup — it only retains the entry when the model's response is substantive (i.e., the model actually used the exec result). This is intentional: if the model responded to the exec output, that context is relevant for future turns. The same design principle applies to regular command output — it becomes part of the conversation history.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Background exec completion notification reaches the model as a heartbeat poll, causing the model to ignore exec output and run heartbeat tasks instead.
- **Real environment tested**: Local gateway with a Qwen3 family model, behind an OpenAI-compatible proxy that logs all LLM requests/responses.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `exec background=true command="sleep 10 && echo 'first done'"`, waited ~10 seconds for the exec completion wake, then inspected the proxy logs.
- **Evidence after fix**:

  User message body sent to the model (exec details inline, visible to security plugins):
  ```
  [user]: [OpenClaw exec completion]

  An async command you ran earlier has completed. The completion details are:

  Exec completed (tide-orb, code 0) :: first done

  User delivery is disabled for this run. Handle the result internally. Continue the task based on the result if needed.
  ```

  Model response:
  ```
  The background command has completed successfully.

  **Result:**
  - Exit code: 0
  - Output: `first done`
  ```

- **Before evidence**:

  Last user message (shows `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` instead of exec details):
  ```
  [user]: [Wed 2026-06-10 17:18 GMT+8] [OpenClaw heartbeat poll]
  ```

  Model response: `HEARTBEAT_OK` (model treated it as a heartbeat poll, ignored exec completion)

- **Observed result after fix**: The `[OpenClaw exec completion]` placeholder prevents the heartbeat behavior. The model sees the exec output in the runtime context and responds appropriately with the command results.
- **What was not tested**: Multi-line exec output, failed commands (non-zero exit), long-running commands with partial output.
- **Proof limitations**: Evidence is from an intercepted LLM request log, not from OpenClaw gateway logs directly. The streaming output was reconstructed from SSE chunks.

## Patch surface

| File | Change | Lines |
|------|--------|-------|
| `src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.ts` | `!deliverToUser` branch shows exec output + "handle internally, continue task" instead of HEARTBEAT_OK; added `hasMissingOutputFailure` sub-branch | +9 |
| `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts` | Remove `lastChannel` param; `deliverToUser: params.canRelayToUser` only | -3 |
| `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts` | Add `EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT` constant | +2 |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts` | Exec-event `transcriptBody` includes exec details from `effectiveBaseBody` | +1 |
| `src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.ts` | `isExecCompletionUserMessage` uses `startsWith` for body format | +1 |
| `src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts` | Test coverage for exec details in body | +9 |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` | Update assertion for new body format | +1 |
| `src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.test.ts` | Update expectations; add `deliverToUser: false` + `hasMissingOutputFailure` test | +13 |

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts`: All tests pass.
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts`: All tests pass.
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts`: All tests pass.
- Added test for `isExecCompletionUserMessage` with exec details appended in body.
- Added test for `isHeartbeatUserMessage` with `EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT` returns `true`.
- Added test for `filterHeartbeatTranscriptArtifacts` removing exec completion pairs alongside heartbeat pairs.
- Added test for `buildReplyPromptEnvelope` with exec-event Provider: `transcriptCommandBody` includes exec details.
- Also ran live gateway test (described in Real behavior proof above) with proxy logging to verify the fix works end-to-end.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` — exec completion notifications now properly reach the model's attention instead of being treated as heartbeat polls.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`
- **What is the highest-risk area?** The transcript filter must correctly identify exec completion messages. The filter now uses `startsWith` instead of exact match, which is correct since exec details are appended after the marker.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The filter checks for the `EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT` prefix with a newline separator before any exec details. Unit tests cover both the bare marker and marker-with-details formats.

## Current review state

- **Next action**: Author ready for review.
- **Waiting on**: None.
- **Comments addressed**: P1 (delivery override) — scoped to exec events + webchat; security gate visibility — exec details now in message body, not hidden in custom_message.

target.title: perf: skip subagent live stream parsing
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip per-chunk live visible-text parsing for subagent runs, which have no live preview consumer.
- Thread `suppressLiveStreamOutput` from the subagent lane through the embedded runner and subscription path.
- Preserve final `message_end` delivery while avoiding live block-buffer writes under suppression.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.types.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/upstream/main` — clean before the final conflict-free rebase; no accepted/actionable findings reported

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Subagent runs skip intermediate live preview parsing during streaming `message_update` events while still accumulating raw deltas and delivering the final assistant text on `message_end`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on the PR branch rebased onto current `upstream/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.types.ts`.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests cover production flag forwarding into the embedded attempt and verify suppressed streaming emits no partial preview while still emitting final assistant text from the same assistant message.

Observed result after fix: Vitest reported 2 files passed / 62 tests passed; oxlint completed successfully for touched files.

What was not tested: No live multi-subagent benchmark was run; this patch is covered with focused unit/integration tests for the affected subscription and runner parameter paths.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add extensible channel identity hook context
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an extensible channel sender/chat context to plugin hook contexts and exposes the same context to `exec`.

It lets a channel plugin pass channel-owned identity metadata, such as Feishu/Lark sender and chat identifiers, without adding more core-specific flat fields. Core defines only the stable base shape:

- `ctx.senderId` and `ctx.chatId` keep the existing flat compatibility fields.
- `ctx.channel.sender.id` mirrors `ctx.senderId` when sender identity is available.
- `ctx.channel.chat.id` mirrors `ctx.chatId` when chat identity is available.
- Channel plugins can extend `ctx.channel.sender` / `ctx.channel.chat` through TypeScript module augmentation.

## Purpose

Some channel integrations need native sender/chat identifiers in plugin hooks and subprocess tools. Flat fields like `senderId` do not scale well because each channel may have different native identifiers. This PR adds a generic channel-owned object so plugins can carry their own metadata while keeping core channel-agnostic.

The immediate use case is Feishu/Lark-style identity metadata that downstream plugin hooks and `exec` integrations need to consume.

## Design considerations

- Keep core plugin-agnostic: core only knows `sender.id` and `chat.id`; richer fields are owned by the channel plugin.
- Preserve existing compatibility: `senderId` / `chatId` remain available where they already make sense.
- Avoid model/tool schema pollution: `exec` receives the serialized context through environment, not through model-generated tool arguments.
- Keep the PR scoped: MCP propagation is intentionally not included because SSE/streamable HTTP MCP needs a separate hook/transport design.
- Avoid non-user leakage: identity fields are omitted for non-user triggers where sender/chat identity is not meaningful.

## Implementation

- Adds `PluginHookChannelContext`, `PluginHookChannelSenderContext`, and `PluginHookChannelChatContext` as extensible SDK-facing types.
- Adds `channelContext` to the channel inbound builder and threads it through auto-reply, queued followups, embedded runs, CLI-backed runs, direct command execution, and hook context builders.
- Ensures inbound `conversation.id` is available as `ChatId`, so common channel-origin runs populate `ctx.chatId` / `ctx.channel.chat.id` without requiring every channel to set a separate native field.
- Passes `channelContext` into coding tools and `exec` defaults.
- Exposes the context to `resolve_exec_env` as `ctx.channel`.
- Adds `OPENCLAW_CHANNEL_CONTEXT` to the final `exec` environment as serialized JSON.
- Covers gateway/node/sandbox exec env propagation and approval metadata with focused tests.

## Follow-up work

- MCP propagation is intentionally deferred. Supporting stdio is straightforward, but SSE / streamable HTTP MCP needs a proper hook or transport-level parameter design.
- Channel plugins can adopt richer `channelContext` fields incrementally through module augmentation.
- Feishu/Lark production fields should be added in a separate channel-plugin PR after this core SDK/exec contract lands.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/channel-context-pr-update.tsbuildinfo --noUnusedLocals false`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: channel-originated plugin hooks and `exec` can receive channel-owned sender/chat metadata through a structured context.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw gateway with Feishu enabled, using a temporary uncommitted Feishu proof patch on top of this PR. The proof patch injected redacted test fields into Feishu `channelContext` and logged hook/exec visibility.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent a Feishu DM that asked the agent to run `uname -a` via `exec`, then inspected gateway logs for `before_agent_start`, `resolve_exec_env`, and final `exec` environment proof lines.

Evidence after fix: the same redacted context shape appeared in all three stages:

```json
{
  "sender": {
    "id": "<feishu-open-id>",
    "testCode1": 1,
    "unionId": "<feishu-union-id>"
  },
  "chat": {
    "id": "<feishu-chat-id>",
    "testCode2": 2
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: `before_agent_start` received `ctx.channel`, `resolve_exec_env` received the same `ctx.channel`, and the final gateway `exec` environment contained both `OPENCLAW_CHANNEL_CONTEXT` and a proof env returned by `resolve_exec_env` with the same serialized channel context.

What was not tested: MCP propagation is not part of this PR and was not validated here.

commands.review_summary:

target.title: fix(discord): prime voice receive with op-5 Speaking burst on join
target.body:
## Summary

- When the bot joined a Discord voice channel and another speaker was already mid-utterance, the receive side could miss the first window of audio because no `Speaking` (op-5) frame had been observed.
- This PR primes the receive side with a synthetic op-5 Speaking burst on join so the receive pipeline starts in the correct state and captures audio from frame 0.
- New file: `extensions/discord/src/voice/receive-prime.ts` (priming helper + test).
- Final back-and-forth voice acceptance was verified separately with live audio.

## Linked context

Closes # (no upstream issue — fix derived from downstream debugging session, spec attached: `specs/discord-voice-receive-debug-2026-05-31.md`)

## Risk

- Scope: `extensions/discord/src/voice/{manager.ts,receive-prime.ts}`.
- Behavior change: receive pipeline now sees a synthetic op-5 frame at join time. Tests cover both bot-joined-while-silent (no audio lost) and bot-joined-mid-speech (audio captured).
- No new dependencies.

## Testing

- New file ships with unit tests.
- Manager.ts integration tested.
- Live voice acceptance done downstream.

target.title: fix(imessage): stage remote media before plugin dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Stage remote inbound media during dispatch before plugin hook and plugin-bound inbound-claim metadata is built.
- Mark successfully staged remote media as already staged so the later agent reply path does not restage the same attachment.
- Add regressions for SCP remote-cache rewriting, plugin-bound Codex claim metadata, and Codex `localImage` turn input.

## Linked context

Closes #91744.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: remote iMessage attachments that start as raw macOS Messages paths are staged before plugin-bound Codex inbound claims are constructed, so the Codex-visible media metadata uses the local remote-cache path instead of `/Users/.../Library/Messages/Attachments/...`.
- Real environment tested: fresh OpenClaw worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-91744-imessage-codex-staged-path` based on live `upstream/main` `9a1f2022b127f27312eeb7f57e42d477bddf2c19`, using the actual dispatch source, message hook mapper, and Codex turn-input module from this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` in the worktree to verify the dispatch ordering in source, build the plugin inbound claim event through the real hook mapper, and convert that event through the real Codex turn-input builder.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the runtime command:

```json
{
  "dispatchOrder": "stage_remote_media_for_dispatch before deriveInboundMessageHookContext",
  "claimMediaPath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg",
  "claimMediaPaths": [
    "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
  ],
  "codexInput": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "what is this?",
      "text_elements": []
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    }
  ],
  "rawMessagesPathVisible": false,
  "localImagePath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the dispatch source now stages remote media before `deriveInboundMessageHookContext()`, the plugin inbound claim metadata carries the staged remote-cache path, and the final Codex turn input contains `localImage` entries for that staged path while `rawMessagesPathVisible` is `false`.
- What was not tested: a live remote Mac/iMessage conversation, because this environment does not have live remote Mac/iMessage credentials.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.media-policy.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/channel-inbound-roots.contract.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

Regression coverage added:

- SCP remote staging success rewrites raw remote iMessage paths to the local remote-cache path.
- Dispatch stages remote iMessage media before plugin-bound Codex inbound claim metadata is built.
- Codex turn input uses the staged remote-cache path as `localImage`.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`
- Highest-risk area: remote media staging now runs earlier in dispatch for remote inbound media, before plugin hooks and plugin-bound claims.
- Mitigation: the new dispatch staging is gated on `MediaRemoteHost`, a session key, inbound media, and `!MediaStaged`; successful staging marks `MediaStaged` to preserve the single-stage contract for later agent processing. Existing path policy and size checks remain in `stageSandboxMedia()`.

## Current review state

- Next action: CI and ClawSweeper review.
- Waiting on: normal PR checks and durable ClawSweeper verdict.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: implements the issue review direction to stage before plugin-bound Codex claim metadata is derived.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: fix(memory): abort search embeddings on tool timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91718 by making the memory_search tool timeout cancel the underlying builtin memory embedding search instead of only racing the outer tool promise.

- Adds an optional `AbortSignal` to the memory search manager options contract.
- Aborts the in-flight memory search when the 15s tool deadline fires.
- Threads the signal into builtin query embedding and the provider fetch operation.
- Stops embedding retries after caller cancellation.
- Keeps caller-aborted searches from activating the configured fallback provider after cancellation.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=0 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager-mock.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree timeout 300s node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree timeout 300s node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager-mock.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-91718-proof.mjs`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

behavior: memory_search timeout cancellation now reaches the builtin embedding provider operation.

environment: Ubuntu/Linux local OpenClaw source checkout on Node 22.22.2, branch `ai-hpc/fix-memory-search-timeout-abort`.

steps:
1. Started a local delayed HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1`.
2. Ran the real `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` implementation with a parent AbortSignal representing the memory_search 15s deadline.
3. Started a provider-style `fetch` against the delayed endpoint using the operation signal.
4. Aborted the parent signal with `memory_search timed out after 15s`.
5. Verified the operation rejected quickly, the provider signal was aborted with the same reason, and the HTTP connection closed before the delayed response.

evidence:
```json
{
  "result": "aborted",
  "errorMessage": "memory_search timed out after 15s",
  "elapsedMs": 219,
  "operationSignalAborted": true,
  "operationSignalReceivedParentReason": true,
  "serverRequestSeen": true,
  "serverConnectionClosedBeforeResponse": true
}
```

observedResult: the caller timeout now cancels the active provider fetch path instead of leaving it to run in the background.

notTested: live external embedding vendor endpoint. The local loopback proof covers the same AbortSignal-to-fetch cancellation path without using real provider credentials.

target.title: feat(gateway): add POST /api/mcp/servers/:id/reload endpoint
target.body:
## Why

When a customer connects a new Composio integration via our UI, the upstream MCP server's tool list changes but Composio's MCP server does not emit a `notifications/tools/list_changed` notification (see ComposioHQ/composio#3118). Without a reload trigger the agent only sees new tools after the session runtime's idle TTL (~10 min) or a config change — unacceptable UX for an enterprise product.

The notification-driven invalidation path (option A) already landed in main and v2026.6.1 per ClawSweeper's review. This PR implements option B: the operator-authenticated HTTP reload endpoint.

## What

### `POST /api/mcp/servers/:serverId/reload`

- **Auth:** same shared-secret / trusted-proxy model as `/tools/invoke` — token/password bearer auth grants full operator access; trusted-proxy auth honours `x-openclaw-scopes`; minimum scope required: `operator.write`
- **Optional targeting:** `X-OpenClaw-Session-Id` header or `?sessionId=` query param target a single active session; when absent, reloads across all active sessions on this gateway instance
- **Effect:** disposes the named server's transport session and invalidates the combined tool catalog so the next `getCatalog()` call reconnects and re-fetches tools from the upstream MCP server

### Method descriptor

Added `mcp.server.reload` to `core-descriptors.ts` with scope `operator.write`, consistent with other mutation endpoints.

### `SessionMcpRuntime.reloadServer(serverName)`

New optional method on the runtime interface. Removes the server's entry from the `sessions` map (forcing reconnect on next use), increments `catalogInvalidationGeneration`, and nulls `catalog`/`catalogInFlight`. The existing `notifications/tools/list_changed` invalidation path uses the same fields so semantics are consistent.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/gateway/mcp-reload-http.ts` | New HTTP route handler |
| `src/gateway/mcp-reload-http.test.ts` | 42 unit tests |
| `src/gateway/methods/core-descriptors.ts` | New method descriptor |
| `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | Route detection + lazy module loading |
| `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-types.ts` | `reloadServer` on `SessionMcpRuntime` type |
| `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts` | `reloadServer` implementation |
| `test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` | Add test file to shard |

## Tests

42 new unit tests covering: path matching, method rejection, 401/403 auth flows, zero/multi/single-session reload, URL-decoded server names, legacy runtime graceful skip, and error paths (404 unknown session, 500 total failure, 200 partial).

## Integration example (Pareto Colabs OAuth callback)

```
POST /api/mcp/servers/composio/reload
Authorization: Bearer <gateway-token>

→ { "ok": true, "serverName": "composio", "sessionCount": 1 }
```

Closes #91556

target.title: fix(agents): infer runtime provider from qualified model ids
target.body:
## Summary
- Infer the effective provider from provider-qualified `modelId` values when `resolveModelRuntimePolicy` is called without an explicit provider.
- Use that inferred provider for provider/model runtime policy lookup and provider-level runtime fallback.
- Add regression coverage for both model-scoped and provider-scoped runtime policies.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed:
`resolveModelRuntimePolicy` already lets `provider: ""` plus `modelId: "anthropic/foo"` match agent-level model runtime entries, but the same provider-qualified model id was not used for `models.providers` lookup. That meant configured provider model runtime policies and provider runtime fallbacks could resolve to `{}` when the caller only supplied the provider-qualified model id.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/provider-runtime-qualified-model`, Node/Vitest through repository scripts.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts --reporter verbose
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-runtime-policy.ts src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts
node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval "<resolveModelRuntimePolicy live proof>"
```

Evidence before fix:
The two new regression cases failed with `expected {} to deeply equal { policy: { id: "claude-cli" }, ... }` for both model-scoped and provider-scoped runtime policies.

Evidence after fix:
- Vitest: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 30 passed (30)`.
- oxfmt: `All matched files use the correct format.`
- Live proof output:
```json
{
  "modelScoped": {
    "policy": {
      "id": "claude-cli"
    },
    "source": "model"
  },
  "providerScoped": {
    "policy": {
      "id": "claude-cli"
    },
    "source": "provider"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
`provider: ""` plus `modelId: "anthropic/claude-opus-4-7"` now resolves both `models.providers.anthropic.models[].agentRuntime` and `models.providers.anthropic.agentRuntime` instead of falling through to `{}`.

What was not tested:
Full embedded CLI execution; this patch is limited to runtime policy resolution and is covered by the targeted runtime policy unit tests plus direct function proof.
